Speaker 1: Okay. I'm going to get to the first tool, which is clarity equals a bigger future. But again, I
want to make sure you understand the value of these tools. This tool is basically
designed to get your attendees, if you're doing this for yourself, to get you focused on
the direction you want to point your ship. In this crazy busy world where we have too
much, we have opportunity overload. We can go down so many different paths, but
really there's only a few things in our life that really move the needle and a few things in
life that move the needle towards where we really want to go, not when we're being
guided by so many outside forces. So what this training does for me and why I love to do
it at the beginning of every Mastermind, to really set the intention is to get your group
to get you focused, laser where you want to go.
In a world where we're always looking where we don't want to go, this is the tool that'll
get your people to sit up and go. That's why I'm in this room. So let's start with your
current status. That's really where you are. You see, if you have a desire to go
someplace, even if you're in your car and you have GPS, you need a starting point. And
for me as the starting point is so important because it's the truth. Listen, you got this
course on how to do Masterminds for a reason. Maybe you're not happy with the
income you have. Maybe you want to make an impact on the world. Maybe you hate
the job you're in. Maybe your company is thriving and you want another level. You want
to learn from the best. All change starts with honesty. You could be feeling pain and the
people that are in your mastermind are feeling pain.
They're uncomfortable in a certain area of their lives, and this is the tool that extracts it
and exposes it. It's like a cut, rip off the Bandaid, expose the cut, put salt on it, like feel it
because pain sometimes causes movement. That's sometimes, always you've done
more in your life out of avoiding pain than you have for pleasure.
So what this tool does is picture you're in a room, you have your Mastermind attendees.
They come in there, they're all like, they know they're there for a specific reason
because they know what you're like. They know what your superpowers are. You've
already targeted them because you know exactly who they are and they're your ideal
clients. So they know that. So they sit in a room and you said to them, "We're going to
start with where are you? The truth, your current situation. What are the pain points?
Why are you here? Do you want to make more money? Do you want more time for your
family? Do you want more time for you? Do you want to work on your health? Do you
want to get in better shape? Do you want to fix your marriage, fix your relationship,
scale your marketing, write a book, do your first real estate deal".
Whatever it is, they're there for a reason and this is the tool to extract it and you're
going to push to them to tell the truth. Don't say, Oh, I just want to expand my business.
No, I'm behind on my mortgage and I hate being late or I have no money in savings or I
want money for my children in case the world goes to hell in a hand basket. This is
where you want to encourage them to tell the truth and as the leader of the
Mastermind, as the leader, be so damn transparent and share yours, share what your
truth is.

today and it was the best year of your life. What does that look like?
Where do you want to point your ship? You see in this section, here's how I teach it. In a
world of chaos, we're like the Tasmanian Devil in the Road Runner. We're spinning so
fast, we got a dust cloud around us. It's hard to set goals, really hard to set goals, but if
we can extract ourselves from the current craziness, with the current dust storm and
move forward one year and look backwards and say, it's the best year of my life. I lost
the weight. I got the girl, I made the money, I scaled the company, I got the real estate
deal. I started my Shopify account. I'm doing ads on Facebook. Whatever it is you're
doing, it's the best year of your life. This is where I encourage people to look back and
start writing down what the best year of your life looks like. Here's what most people
don't realize, and I've seen this at every level of my Masterminds.
So many of us, so many of them, get sucked into what they don't want out of life. They
know if you ask if they're in a mastermind, if you want, and I do this sometimes in the
exercise, it's tell me all the things you don't want. What aren't your goals? The opposite
of your goals? They say, Oh, I don't want the stress of my marketing department, or I
don't want the stress of sales. I don't want my wife to nag me or, I don't want my
husband not to listen to me. I don't want to work for this boss. Like everybody knows
what they don't want, but then I'll stop them really quick and I'll say, well, where is it
that you want to go, hurry up, tell me guys, where do you want to go? And everybody
will say, the majority will say, Oh, really good question or, I'm not sure, or let me think
about him.
So it just shows me that the world, we're running so fast, we're all in Lamborghinis
without our GPS on. We're just driving fast. We're running on a treadmill expecting to
get somewhere. And I let them know that. And as you watch when you do this exercise,
everybody in the room, whether it's virtual or live, will go, wow, I know all the things I

don't want and this is where we're going to get clarity. So they can point their ship in the

So I go through a little example of how to get them clarity on what they do want, not
what they don't. So that's the first step. And I tell a story, you can steal this story, but
one of my partners, Ethan Willis is a leader in his church. Him and a bunch of dads take
20 kids, between 15 and 17 years old, white water rafting in Colorado as a church
adventure trip. They get there and it rained for days prior to them being there. They
were a nervous wreck when the rapids were a five out of five. No, like I think we're
going to cancel. But the guide came out and he said, dad's relax, boys get in the raft, he
goes we're gonna be fine today. You see this finger? It's the positive point. You see
when I point, I want you boys to focus all your energy where I point and paddle your
guts out and I promise you we'll be safe.
I've never lost anyone, he joked, that kind of thing. He said, "Here's what I did when I
first started becoming a guide, we'd be in a raft and I'd see a really bad rapid and I go,
boys don't go to that rapid. We'll flip over. I'd see a tree that was down and oh, my God,
don't hit the tree. I'd see a rock in the way. Don't hit the rock." And he said, "What
would everybody do? They would turn and they'd look at the rock, look at the rapid,
look at the tree, we'd run right into it and flip over". And I realized that that moment
that what we focus on is where we go. And so many of you in this room are focusing on
what you don't want in your business and your life and your health and your
relationships, in anything that fill in the blanks, and you're just getting more of it.
He said, the guide turned to him and said, "But ever since I pointed my finger where it is,
I want you guys to to go. And you put all your energy, all your effort, all your rowing into
that. We go right by the rock and you don't even know it's there". So today with this
exercise, what I want to do is help you guys get your bigger future. Where is it that you
want to go so we can put all of our energy, so we can focus our ship in one direction. So
you can avoid all the things that aren't building your life, your bigger future. And that's
what this section is. And something I learned from one of my mentors, Dan Sullivan, was
in today's crazy busy world we're spinning and running so fast. We have a dust cloud
around us, like the Tasmanian Devil in the Road Runner cartoons of the olden days.
I said olden days. So that just shows how old I am, right? So, but we're so busy that it's
